title: Navitas Semiconductor Announces Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results
text: Navitas Semiconductor reported total revenue of $10.1 million for Q3 2025, down significantly from $21.7 million in Q3 2024 and $14.5 million in Q2 2025. CEO Chris Allexandre stated, We are executing a strategic pivot from consumer and mobile markets to these fast-growing, more profitable, more sustainable higher-power segments. Navitas announced Navitas 2.0, a repositioning toward high-power semiconductor markets including AI data centers, performance computing, energy infrastructure, and industrial electrification—moving away from lower-margin consumer and mobile products. The company posted a GAAP operating loss of $19.4 million versus $29.0 million in Q3 2024. Non-GAAP operating loss improved to $11.5 million from $12.7 million year-over-year. Navitas maintains $150.6 million in cash and equivalents as of September 30, 2025. Q4 2025 revenue is expected between $6.75–$7.25 million, with non-GAAP gross margin of approximately 38.5% and operating expenses around $15.0 million.
